values: The login page loads quickly and without layout issues.
Use the provided Playwright admin test login.
You land in the dashboard without errors or confusing redirects.
Look at the dashboard, sidebar, and top header.
You immediately understand where to begin the workday.
Navigate to Orders from the sidebar.
The screen loads without errors and feels like the right starting point for order entry.
Select a customer, add address details, and fill out notes.
The form is understandable and does not feel cluttered.
Choose an item that currently has zero or negative stock.
The item can still be added to the order.
Click different products in the combobox and try searching.
Selecting an item never sends you to a Something went wrong screen.
Submit one order with Create Order only.
The order saves and appears as pending.
Submit one order with Create + Send.
The order is sent to processing and the print popup opens.
From Dashboard, open Orders Needing Weights.
The queue is easy to find and understand.
Type weights for the order line items.
Weight fields are easy to use and save successfully.
Use Print Invoice after saving weights.
The print view opens cleanly even without a physical printer.
Navigate to Invoices from the sidebar.
Search by customer or invoice info.
The new invoice is easy to locate.
Open or preview the printed invoice output.
The invoice looks usable and contains the expected details.
Navigate to Inventory from the sidebar.
The page loads cleanly and key actions are visible.
A printable count sheet opens without popup errors.
Use Restock Item on a valid inventory item.
The success message is clear and the action feels safe.
Use Apply Adjustment with a positive or negative delta.
The adjustment works and the wording makes sense.
Move stock from one SKU to another.
The transfer succeeds and the flow is easy to understand.
Submit a spoilage entry with reason and notes.
Spoilage is easy to post and clearly labeled.
Navigate to Customers from the sidebar.
The page loads quickly and feels organized.
Find the new customer and open View / Edit.
Customer data is easy to review and update.
Navigate to Routes from the sidebar.
The route dashboard loads without errors.
Add route name, driver, and notes.
Creating a route is quick and obvious.
Modify the route information after creation.
Search for a customer and add them to the route.
The stop add flow is easy to follow.
Use the batch add from pending orders section.
Pending-order stop add works and makes operational sense.
Close the editor, then reopen the route.
The route still shows the expected stops and details.
Think through order intake, weights, invoice, inventory, and route prep.
The overall sequence feels natural for actual staff use.
Write down any step where you paused or felt uncertain.
Pain points are clear enough to describe and prioritize.
